claims.claim: `/search` is the primary server-rendered listing discovery entry point.
`/search` parses URL params through `parseSearchParams`.
`/search` applies server-side rate limiting before the main search path.
The search page uses the V2 search path first and still has a legacy fallback path.
Exact V2/search-doc versus legacy fallback branching and field parity remain incomplete.
URL search params are treated as the canonical committed search input.
Text searches without usable bounds can enter a bounds-required or location-required state.
Filter controls write search state through URL/query navigation and focused desktop filter/URL behavior has passing E2E coverage; broader cross-project filter coverage remains unrun.
Load-more pagination is implemented through `SearchResultsClient` and `fetchMoreListings`.
Focused desktop sort/load-more pagination reset behavior has passing E2E coverage, while broader root pagination/sort and map-bounds reset coverage remains incomplete.
The `/search` layout hosts persistent map/list providers and the map wrapper.
`PersistentMapWrapper` can consume V2 map data and independently fetch `/api/map-listings`.
The map component contains clustering, marker, selected-listing preview, empty-state, and error-state code.

Desktop map preference, focused list-backed map parity, marker/list focus, pan/zoom URL state, mobile map/list behavior, and map error/a11y behavior have passing browser coverage; V1-only map API mock cases and provider terminology remain incomplete.
claims.citation: README.md; 02-user-flows.md; 12-gaps-unknowns-and-questions.md G001, G005, G011; evidence-register.md C031, C056

claims.claim: `/api/map-listings` requires valid bounds or derived bounds and blocks unbounded map scans.
Map provider terminology is not yet fully settled because code/config evidence points to MapLibre/Stadia while some comments mention Mapbox.
Listing cards link to listing detail pages and expose favorite plus show-on-map controls.
A direct contact-host action from search cards was not verified.
The favorite button posts to `/api/favorites` and the API requires authentication for POST.
Logged-out favorite POST handling redirects the client to `/login`, not a callback URL.
`/api/favorites` GET is public but returns private user-specific saved ids only when authenticated.
Saved search is documented as an entry point/server action, with checkout/paywall details only partially traced.
`/api/search/v2`, `/api/search/listings`, `/api/search/facets`, `/api/map-listings`, `/api/geocoding/autocomplete`, and `/api/favorites` are covered by API contract rows.
The final docs now include compact API response, public payload, error, and cache/header tables for the main search/map/favorites contracts; generated OpenAPI-level schemas remain out of scope.
claims.citation: 05-api-contracts.md; 12-gaps-unknowns-and-questions.md G008; phase-4/02-api-data-flow.md; src/lib/search/types.ts; src/lib/search-types.ts; src/lib/search/search-response.ts; src/lib/public-cache/headers.ts

claims.claim: Public search/map cache behavior is separated from private favorite responses, and Phase 10/11/12 evidence records passing fixed public-payload PII scans for the main search/list/map/listings payloads.
The data model references listings, locations, saved listings, saved searches, search projections, semantic projections, and query snapshots.
Search-doc, semantic search, saved-search, projection, and contact-first listing fields have related migrations, but migration SQL has not been fully line-mapped.
Booking-mode/search-doc fields are treated as code/data history and not documented as active booking UX.
`Search this area` is not documented as current behavior because only a removed-flow comment was found.
Focused result/error/loading/network resilience states and map error/a11y paths have passing E2E coverage, with V1-only map API mock cases still skipped in V2 mode.

The test matrix lists existing search/map/unit/API/E2E tests, marks the narrow smoke, filter, pagination, desktop map, results-state, URL-state, anonymous saved-listing redirect, mobile map/list, search error-resilience, and map error/a11y specs as passed, and leaves broader tests as `Not run`.
The manifest now records grouped test-family entries rather than an exact per-file test index.
The docs now distinguish committed/runtime evidence from remaining local-only dirty worktree discovery entries.

claims.claim: Phase 10 Playwright runtime verification was initially blocked because local Postgres was unavailable at `localhost:5433`.
After Docker/Postgres became available, the first `/search` smoke test reached the browser, rendered 57 listings, and failed on unhandled page error `Invalid or unexpected token`.
A later rerun of the narrow desktop-anonymous `/search` smoke test passed with `1 passed (27.0s)` after rendering 57 listings.

claims.discrepancy: Historical claim remains true for the original scan, but it is superseded by the fixed payload scan evidence in C050.

claims.claim: PII scan triage classifies rounded public coordinates and image URL matches as likely scanner false positives, `list.fullItems.*.location.lat/lng` as a potential real privacy risk, and group/context keys as requiring a privacy decision.
claims.citation: public-payload-pii-triage.md; evidence-register.md C048; src/lib/search/transform.ts:L99-L120; src/lib/search/public-coordinates.ts:L1-L18; src/lib/maps/sanitize-map-listings.ts:L87-L164; src/lib/search/search-v2-service.ts:L1127-L1130; src/lib/search/types.ts:L134-L138; src/lib/search-types.ts:L56-L81

claims.claim: Search V2 `fullItems` is used by the current client and multiple response producers, so removal without a migration is likely contract-breaking.
After the P0 public payload fix, real captured public payloads from `/api/search/v2`, `/api/search/listings`, `/api/map-listings`, and `/api/listings` scanned cleanly.
claims.citation: runtime-verification.md; evidence-register.md C050; public-payload-pii-triage.md; command output from `pnpm run scan:public-payload-pii -- /tmp/roomshare-payload-search-v2.json /tmp/roomshare-payload-search-listings.json /tmp/roomshare-payload-map-listings.json /tmp/roomshare-payload-listings.json`

coverageGaps.gap: The narrow smoke, filter/URL, sort/load-more pagination, desktop map, results-state, URL-state, anonymous/authenticated saved-listing, mobile map/list, search error-resilience, map error/a11y, focused API/unit Jest, PII scanner fixture, real payload PII scan, and release-gate commands now have evidence, but V1-only map API browser mock cases and non-gate broader E2E coverage remain not runtime-verified.

Tests are discovered and grouped in the manifest; focused search/map harness commands, focused API/unit Jest, the P0 privacy suite, typecheck, the full search release gate, scanner fixture checks, and the real fixed payload scan have passed. Non-gate broader E2E commands have not passed in this session.

The desktop search harness pagination spec verifies sort reset, unique appended cards, and retryable failure/rate-limit states, but root pagination/sort specs and map-bounds reset coverage remain unrun.
The concrete search/list/map public payload leak is fixed and verified by real captured payload scanning. A deterministic no-arg payload-capture wrapper is still missing for the package scanner gate.
The docs now include compact field/cache/error tables for the main contracts, but they are not generated OpenAPI schemas and do not enumerate every optional debug-only field combination.

Desktop, mobile, and map error/a11y behavior have passing search-harness coverage with mocked map resources, but provider terminology and V1-only map API mock cases still need verification because comments/config mix Mapbox, MapLibre, and Stadia terms.
Search-card direct contact was not found; listing detail/contact-host reachability should be verified in the separate contact-host feature pass.

Docs distinguish committed/runtime evidence from local-only dirty worktree discovery entries; reviewers should preserve or stage remaining related source files before treating the whole local branch as stable.
The manifest uses grouped test-family entries; a machine-readable per-file test index can be generated later if needed.
